rationale: All four patch findings (CR-001 through CR-004) are clearly resolved in the 42-line fix-up diff: credential_type filter added, multiple-integration warning added, isUpdate gate skip added, optional chain applied. CR-005 (decision_needed) is superseded — design decision confirmed that fallback on all SSO errors (including cancellation) is intentional per the spec error-handling table; no code change required. No new high-risk issues introduced. Fix-up commit passed pre-commit hooks (ESLint zero warnings, typecheck, related unit tests).

business_review.text: During setup, the system detects when the selected project already has a LiteLLM integration created.
business_review.notes: Filter uses project_name only; any integration type triggers enforcement, not just LiteLLM-type integrations.

business_review.text: If LiteLLM integration exists, the setup flow enforces its usage for CLI configuration.
business_review.notes: Auto-selects litellm correctly when triggered, but trigger condition is too broad (wrong type filter); also silently picks the first when multiple integrations match.

business_review.text: User cannot complete CLI setup without providing the required LiteLLM key.
business_review.notes: Validator re-prompts on blank input; 'not-required' fallback is suppressed in enforcement mode.

business_review.text: Validation message clearly explains why setup cannot continue without LiteLLM credentials.
business_review.notes: Header prints integration alias and portal hint; validator message cites the CodeMie portal.

business_review.text: The enforced flow does not affect projects that do not have LiteLLM integration created.
business_review.notes: Projects with no integrations fall through correctly, but projects with non-LiteLLM integrations are incorrectly gated into the LiteLLM setup path.

business_review.text: No invalid CLI configuration state can be saved for projects where LiteLLM integration is mandatory.
business_review.notes: Enforcement mode prevents empty key and 'not-required' fallback from being returned or saved.

standards_review.notes: Commits use allowed types (feat, fix, test) with cli/providers scopes per commitlint.config.cjs; pre-commit hooks passed.
Fix-up diff adds no new console.log(); prior partial on main diff stands (established setup.ts UX pattern).
CR-004 fix applies optional chain to enforcement mode apiKey; prior partial carried forward from final round.

finding_status.notes: Filter now `allIntegrations.filter(i => i.project_name === project && i.credential_type === 'LiteLLM')` — non-LiteLLM integrations no longer trigger the enforcement path.
logger.warn added when projectIntegrations.length > 1, naming the selected alias. Deterministic selection (first element) is now documented at the warn site.
Ternary `isUpdate ? { enforced: false as const } : await detectLiteLLMEnforcement()` skips SSO gate on all update flows.
`answers.apiKey?.trim() ?? ''` applied — optional chain prevents TypeError on undefined in headless/CI environments.
Design decision confirmed: falling back on all SSO errors (including user-initiated cancellation) is intentional per the spec error-handling table. No code change required.
